evidence.scientific_clarity: Left-to-right workflow (INPUT→RETRIEVAL→GENERATION→OUTPUT→RESULT) clearly conveys the retrieval-augmented diffusion pipeline; embedded headline '×2 hit rate' and three metric callouts (+18%, 60%, R²>0.9) make the claim graspable in <5 seconds.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' both set; Arial/Helvetica stack used throughout (confirmed in PDF: CIDFontType2 /GOFYPY+Arial-BoldMT, ArialMT, Arial-ItalicMT embedded); no DejaVu Sans fallback visible; smallest text is 7px which is borderline but acceptable at 300 dpi.
evidence.palette: Cool-family only (slate-blue, teal, mint, grey); no red/green directional confusion; all hues are ColorBrewer-compatible desaturated tones; ≤4 distinct hues as required; colorblind-safe (no red/green pair).
evidence.layout_density: Pipeline zone occupies most of the canvas well; however the bottom metrics strip sits low with noticeable vertical gap between the pipeline elements and the strip, and the bar chart is pushed to the far right with some unused horizontal space between the molecule and the chart.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes specific numerical claims; no cross-references; however the bar chart y-axis starts at 0.9 (below baseline bar base) which could draw a Reviewer 2 comment about truncated axis misleading the ×2 impression; no legend needed (bars are labeled).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 63 kB with embedded TrueType subsets (CIDFontType2 with FontFile2 streams) confirming real vector output with selectable/editable text; pdf.fonttype=42 confirmed in script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (63 kB, PDF 1.4, 1 page), figure.png (265 kB, 3660×1859 RGBA), figure.svg (present, non-trivial), caption.tex (non-trivial with numerical claims), gen_figure.py (325 lines, fully reproducible) — all required files present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption leads with the headline finding ('doubles hit rate'), then quantifies three supporting claims (+18% Vina docking score, 60% data efficiency, R²>0.9 scaling law across 5 model sizes) — specific, numerical, and self-contained.

overall_strengths: Fully reproducible matplotlib script with pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' producing real embedded-font vector output; Arial stack confirmed in PDF internals.

Scientific narrative is immediately legible: workflow pattern with zone labels, embedded headline sentence, and a three-metric strip collectively communicate all three paper claims without requiring the paper body.
overall_weaknesses: Bar chart y-axis range (0.9–2.15) truncates below the baseline bar's base value, which could be flagged as a misleading axis by a reviewer — a zero-based or clearly annotated axis would be safer.

Moderate vertical dead space between the pipeline row and the metrics strip reduces layout density; the bar chart panel is also somewhat isolated at the far right with a gap after the molecule element.
